item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ok, I like it, the concept is solid the masterful mix of hinting at what he did without actually telling the reader is good I'm going to guess based on Jason's actions and the history of the object given he was responsible for the house fire? my only issue is the 2nd log, In the 1st log I can brush off the behavior of the researchers as just their characters, in the 2nd one I doubt any foundation personnel could get away with the outburst Dr. Greves gives also Jason's character is weird. he's very distressed and vulnerable one moment, invoking sympathy, and the next he's a boomers version of an edgy 14-year-old give it a read through again and polish up the dialogue. But since I enjoyed it so much I'll give you an upvote in good faith that you will fix it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just as Ihp already said, the dialogue is wayyyy to edgy to sound like a 14-year old. That´s a -1 from me.

item.title: Has Great Potential
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is definitely one of the better ones that I have seen in recent months, and I congratulate you on your efforts! The story was intriguing, and the synopsis clear. My only issues include that of the final addendum, which concludes the story on a rather short note, which is a shame as I believe that this could have had some more story to it. Dialogue and Transcripts are all right, though I would suggest that you go over it again and make some edits to make the language more believable. Other than that, I have no complaints.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice concept, but it's gonna have to be neutral for me. Really like the idea, but it was a bit overly edgy and the note at the end didn't make sense. It's basically a thinly veiled version of hell, it's never clarified what he did, and it eventually just turns into "Yeah just say some cryptic stuff"

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded:
sorry but didn't Nickthebrick1 say the exact same things?
i think the last note doesn't wrap the story up effectively in my opinion i think the story would be better without it as it just feels like a useless afterword

item.title: Staff Post - Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: RhettyBoy, this sort of feedback doesn't really help the author. Please try and be more detailed in your feedback if you're going to leave comments - Why does this not work for you?

item.title: dialogue needs work
item.content:encoded: also the last addendum seems useless to the story

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with this. Not a fan of the dialogue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jason's dialog is unrealistic and overly edgy, and not in the sense of '14-year-old who is trying too hard to sound cool'. Combine that with this being a very simplistic version of Hell, and it's a downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but maybe you should remove the last note at the end? It doesn't seem to add much to the story in my opinion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't read the article yet, but just wanted to give you a heads up: the ACS and the image are overlapping. You can fix this by adding ----- between the two code sets, which will add a horizonal rule between the two. Or, you can just add @@@@ in between to add a blank space.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really liked the article up until the 05 note at the end, which seemed a bit jarring in how quickly it wrapped up the story. I'm unsure if I'm supposed to read the mental stress and suicides as a property of the object, or just last-minute shock factor.

Either way, the rest of the article is solid enough to earn an enthusiastic +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed some little errors and gave this a +1
Love it!

item.title: All of my Critters
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to,
cybersqyd, PlaguePJP, Grigori Karpin, WizzBlizz.

I own the rights to this image, the image is a photo taken and edited by myself.

Thank you all, it was much appreciated.

EDIT (12/26/2020):
I understand that a lot of people were unsatisfied with this SCP. I've made a few changes, however.

  • A few dialogue changes for both Dr. Graves and SCP-5755-D
  • A change in some terminology
  • A slight change in the presentation of the logs
  • Change of an important story detail (SCP-5755-D now recognizes the rest as his family, but not quite)
  • Addition to the ending, as it ended a bit out of no-where

I understand my SCP isn't A-grade material but a lot of feedback and a stop from my laziness helped improve my SCP just a bit more. Hopefully, those who have -1 might change their mind? I don't have many expectations about that aspect.
